A.text:
## Overview
Group A is settled, and with a surprise: host Mexico advanced in first place with a perfect campaign, and South Africa struck a blow in the final round to take second place. Mexico finished with 9 points and no goals conceded; South Africa clinched a direct spot with 4 points. South Korea, with 3, dropped to third and still dreams of one of the eight best-third-place spots; the Czech Republic (1) is eliminated.
## Undisputed leader
Javier Aguirre's Mexico was supreme: three wins in three matches, 6 goals scored and an impenetrable defense — they conceded no goals in the group stage. They beat South Africa (2-0), South Korea (1-0) and thrashed the Czech Republic 3-0 in the finale, with goals from Mateo Chávez, Julián Quiñones and Álvaro Fidalgo. First place with authority and three clean sheets.
## The turnaround for the 2nd spot
The big story was South Africa's direct qualification. Bottom of the table after two rounds, they needed to beat South Korea — and won 1-0, with a goal from Thapelo Maseko in the 63rd minute, in a head-to-head clash for the spot. The result took South Africa to 4 points and pushed South Korea into third place, with 3 points and a negative goal difference (-1). The South Koreans are not eliminated, but they depend on results from the other groups to figure among the eight best third-place finishers.
## Verdict
Mexico is safely through in first, with a flawless campaign; South Africa is the pleasant surprise in second, after deciding it on fine margins against South Korea. South Korea, in third, remains alive — but in a fragile position — in the race for a best-third-place spot; the Czech Republic is eliminated. Group A is closed, with South Korea's fate still in the hands of the other groups.

B.text:
## Overview
Group B is closed with Switzerland in first and Canada in second, both qualified for the Round of 32. Switzerland finished unbeaten, with 7 points; Canada, the co-host, took 4 and the best goal difference in the group (+5). Bosnia also collected 4 points, but a negative goal difference (-1) pushed them into third place — and they remain ALIVE in the race for one of the eight best-third-place spots. Qatar finished bottom, with 1 point, eliminated.
## The qualifiers
Switzerland was the force of the group: after drawing with Qatar in the opener (1-1), they thrashed Bosnia (4-1) and overcame Canada 2-1 in the final round to secure first place. Canada shone in attack — thrashing Qatar 6-0 and holding the best goal difference in the group (+5), with Jonathan David standing out — but stumbled against the Swiss and finished second.
## Bosnia and the best-third-place spot
Second place — er, the head-to-head order was decided on goal difference: Bosnia and Canada tied on 4 points, but Canada's difference (+5) comfortably outdid Bosnia's (-1). Even in third, Bosnia is NOT eliminated: in the 48-team World Cup, the eight best third-place finishers also advance to the Round of 32, and Bosnia's 4 points is a competitive mark. With only three of the twelve groups closed, Bosnia's fate will only be known after the final round of the remaining groups. Their 3-1 win over Qatar in the finale — their only win — could weigh in their favor.
## Verdict
Switzerland is through in first and on a roll; Canada advances in second despite the stumble at home. Bosnia, in third, remains in suspense: it depends on results from the other groups to confirm a spot among the eight best third-place finishers. Qatar, with 1 point and 10 goals conceded, was eliminated at the bottom.

C.text:
## Overview
Group C is decided at the top: Brazil and Morocco advance to the Round of 32. The two leaders finished level on 7 points, but the Seleção took first on goal difference (+6 against +3). Scotland collected 3 points and finished third, still with a — remote — chance of figuring among the eight best third-place finishers; Haiti finished bottom, without scoring a point, eliminated.
## The qualifiers
Brazil got going after the opening draw with Morocco (1-1): they thrashed Haiti and Scotland 3-0, with Vinícius Jr (4 goals) and Matheus Cunha (3) standing out, and finished with the best defense in the group. Morocco kept up their consistency — beating Scotland (1-0) and Haiti (4-2) — and advanced in second.
## The situation of the rest
Scotland did beat Haiti (1-0), but lost to Morocco and Brazil and finished third with 3 points and a -3 goal difference. They are not mathematically eliminated — the eight best third-place finishers also advance — but the negative goal difference and an unproductive attack (1 goal) leave the Scots in a delicate position, rooting for slip-ups in the other groups. Haiti suffered three defeats and is eliminated, even though they scored twice against Morocco.
## Verdict
Brazil is through in first and on a roll, with a sharp attack and a solid defense; Morocco advances firmly in second. Scotland, in third, still has a breath of life in the race for a best-third-place spot, but depends on others' results; Haiti is eliminated. Group C is closed.

D.text:
## Overview
Group D is closed and the outcome already stamped. The United States finished first with 6 points, running away early — 4-1 over Paraguay and 2-0 over Australia — before stumbling in a match with nothing at stake, a 3-2 loss to Turkey in the final round. Behind the leader, Australia and Paraguay tied on 4 points and Turkey, even winning at the end, fell short.
## Who advances
United States in first, a comfortable qualification despite the ending. Australia secured second place with 4 points and a goal difference of zero (a 2-0 win over Turkey, a loss to the USA and a 0-0 with Paraguay), edging Paraguay on goal difference. Paraguay, also with 4 points but a -2 difference, finishes third.
## Best-third-place spot
Paraguay is not eliminated: they finished third and enter the race for one of the eight best-third-place spots. By the provisional count of third-place finishers, Paraguay sits inside the cut with 4 points, but the -2 difference leaves the position fragile — everything depends on results from the remaining groups to confirm (or not) qualification.
## Eliminated
Turkey is the only side eliminated from the group. They collected 3 points thanks to their win over the United States in the final round (3-2), but the losses to Australia (0-2) and Paraguay (0-1) cost them dearly: they finished fourth, with no chance at a best-third-place spot.
## Verdict
The United States and Australia have stamped the two direct spots. Paraguay lives on rooting for the other groups to confirm a best-third-place spot, and Turkey bows out with the bitter feeling of having beaten the leader too late.

E.text:
## Overview
Group E is closed with Germany in first and Ivory Coast in second, both qualified for the Round of 32. Germany and Ivory Coast finished level on 6 points, but the Mannschaft took the top spot on goal difference (+6 against +2). Ecuador, with 4 points, finished third and remains alive in the fight for one of the eight best-third-place spots. Curaçao, making their World Cup debut, finished bottom with 1 point.
## The qualifiers
Julian Nagelsmann's Germany was overwhelming in attack — 10 goals scored, the best in the group — with a thrashing of Curaçao (7-1) and a hard-fought win over Ivory Coast (2-1). They stumbled in the final round, surprised by Ecuador (1-2), but had already all but secured their spot and first place. Ivory Coast grew as the competition went on: after losing to the Germans, they beat Ecuador (1-0) and Curaçao (2-0), the latter with a decisive brace from Nicolas Pépé that stamped second place.
## The third-place finisher
Ecuador had an up-and-down campaign: they started without scoring (a loss to Ivory Coast and a 0-0 with Curaçao), but exploded at the right time, beating the already-qualified Germany 2-1, with Nilson Angulo and Gonzalo Plata. The 4 points leave La Tri provisionally inside the group of eight best third-place finishers — now it's a matter of rooting for results from the remaining groups to confirm the spot.
## Verdict
Germany is through in first, though with a warning after the final stumble; Ivory Coast advances in second, buoyed by Pépé's goals. Ecuador breathes in the best-third-place race, while Curaçao bows out with the honor of the historic point earned on their World Cup debut. Group E is closed.

F.text:
## Overview
Group F is decided. The Netherlands finished first with 7 points and a +6 goal difference, Japan took the second spot with 5, and Sweden, third with 4, remains alive in the race for one of the eight best-third-place spots. Tunisia, without a point and with the leakiest defense in the tournament (12 goals conceded), was eliminated.
## Dutch leaders
The Oranje put together the best campaign in the group: a 2-2 draw with Japan in the opener, a 5-1 thrashing of Sweden and a 3-1 over Tunisia in the final round. Ten goals scored and only four conceded — they finished clear at the top and take the seeding advantage into the Round of 32.
## Japan secured
Japan did not lose in the group: two draws (2-2 with the Netherlands and 1-1 with Sweden) and the strongest result of the final round, 4-0 over Tunisia. Five points, second place and a spot secured in the Round of 32.
## Sweden by a thread
Third with 4 points and a goal difference of zero, Sweden hands its fate to the math of the other groups: for now they sit provisionally inside the cut of eight best third-place finishers, but it's a fragile position, confirmed only when the remaining groups close. They thrashed Tunisia 5-1, but the 5-1 loss to the Netherlands ate up the goal difference they now miss.
## Verdict
The Netherlands and Japan advance comfortably; Tunisia is eliminated. Sweden did their part by not losing to Japan in the decider, but they remain dependent — alive in the fight for a best-third-place spot, but with no guarantees.

G.text:
## Overview
Group G is decided. Belgium and Egypt stamped their spots in the Round of 32, with the Red Devils on top on goal difference after an overwhelming reaction in the final round. Iran finished third, but remains alive; New Zealand was the only side eliminated.
## The Belgian explosion
After a stalled start — 1-1 with Egypt and 0-0 with Iran — Belgium came alive at the right time and thrashed New Zealand 5-1, with two from Trossard and one from De Bruyne. They reached 5 points and a +4 goal difference, enough for first place. Egypt matched them point for point: also 5 points, but a +2 difference after the 1-1 with Iran, and finished second.
## Iran alive, New Zealand out
Iran drew all three of their matches (0-0, 1-1 and 2-2), collected 3 points with a goal difference of zero and finished third. Note: they are not eliminated — they're in the fight for one of the eight best-third-place spots and appear provisionally inside, dependent on the other groups. New Zealand, with just 1 point and ten goals conceded, is the only side to bid farewell to the World Cup from this group.
## Verdict
Belgium and Egypt advance, with Belgium securing first on goal difference. Iran stays in the race as a provisional best third-place finisher; New Zealand is eliminated.

H.text:
## Overview
Group H is closed, and with one of the biggest surprises of the first stage: Spain went through in first with total authority, Cape Verde made history by qualifying in second, and Uruguay was quite the disappointment, falling as early as the group stage.
## Flawless Spain
Spain was by far the best team in the group: 7 points, no goals conceded and a +5 goal difference. After a 0-0 with Cape Verde in the opener, they thrashed Saudi Arabia 4-0 and beat Uruguay 1-0, finishing first with room to spare and the most solid defense in the group.
## Cape Verde surprises, Uruguay sinks
Cape Verde secured a historic qualification with three draws (0-0 with Spain, 2-2 with Uruguay and 0-0 with Saudi Arabia): a disciplined defense and the coolness to grind out the points that were enough for second place. Uruguay was the opposite — two draws and the loss to Spain left La Celeste in third with just 2 points and soccer well below expectations.
## Verdict
Spain and Cape Verde advance to the Round of 32. Uruguay, third with 2 points, is out: there are already nine third-place finishers from closed groups with 3 points or more and, since only the eight best third-place finishers qualify, La Celeste cannot reach the cut even with Groups J and K still in play. Saudi Arabia, bottom with 2 points and a -4 goal difference, is also eliminated.

I.text:
## Overview
Group I is closed with France dominating from start to finish. The French put together a perfect campaign — three wins, 10 goals scored and only 2 conceded — and nailed down first place with 9 points. Norway took the second spot, and the big turnaround came from Senegal: scoreless after two rounds, they rehabilitated themselves with a rout in the final round and remain alive in the fight for a best-third-place spot.
## Undisputed leader
France gave no quarter: 3-1 over Senegal, 3-0 over Iraq and 4-1 over Norway in the finale. A 100% record, a +8 goal difference and the best campaign in the group. A comfortable qualification in first.
## The second spot
Norway secured second place with 6 points. They beat Iraq (4-1) and Senegal (3-2) before falling to France in the final round. A +1 goal difference — enough to finish second, ahead of Senegal on the tiebreaker.
## Senegal's turnaround
After losing their first two (1-3 to France and 2-3 to Norway), Senegal looked out. But the 5-0 rout of Iraq in the final round took them to 3 points and a +2 goal difference — and placed them, provisionally, inside the cut of eight best third-place finishers. They are not eliminated: they finished third and depend on results from the other groups to confirm the spot.
## Eliminated
Iraq is the only side eliminated. They lost all three matches, scored only 1 goal and conceded 12 — including the 0-5 to Senegal that sealed the bottom of the group.
## Verdict
France and Norway move on, with the French as one of the forces of the tournament. Senegal was reborn at the right time and roots for the other groups to confirm a best-third-place spot, while Iraq bows out without a point.

J.text:
## Overview
Group J is closed — and, with the entire first stage now wrapped up, its story has a happy ending for three of the four participants. Argentina went through like a steamroller: 9 points, three wins and the best goal difference of the entire group stage (+7). Austria secured second place with 4 points, on fine goal-difference margins. Algeria, also with 4, finished third and confirmed a spot as one of the eight best third-place finishers. Jordan, without a point in three matches, bows out at the bottom, eliminated.
## The Messi show
Argentina was supreme and had one name above the rest: Lionel Messi. The star scored six of Argentina's eight goals in the group stage — a hat-trick in the 3-0 over Algeria, two in the 2-0 against Austria and one more in the 3-1 over Jordan, a match rounded out by Giovani Lo Celso and Lautaro Martínez. With only one goal conceded in three matches, Lionel Scaloni's side reaches the knockouts as one of the tournament's great forces.
## Austria and Algeria go through together
The 3-3 between Algeria and Austria, in the final round, decided second place. The Algerians led 3-2 with two goals from Riyad Mahrez (the second in the 90th+3'), but Sasa Kalajdzic equalized in the 90th+6' and took from Algeria the win that would have meant second place. With the point, Austria — who had beaten Jordan 3-1 — finished second on goal difference (0 against -2). Algeria dropped to third, but the five goals they scored proved decisive: with the group stage over, they appear among the eight best third-place finishers and are qualified for the knockouts.
## Verdict
Argentina is through in first with a 100% record and Messi at the very highest level; Austria advances in second on fine goal-difference margins; and Algeria, third with 4 points, confirmed their presence in the Round of 32 as one of the best third-place finishers. Three of the group's four teams remain alive in the World Cup — only Jordan, without a point, fell by the wayside.

K.text:
## Overview
Group K is closed and the final picture has two qualifiers and a drama for the third spot. Colombia finished on top with 7 points (2W, 1D), followed by Portugal, who collected 5 (1W, 2D) — both secured in the knockouts (Round of 32). Uzbekistan, without a point and with 11 goals conceded, finishes bottom, eliminated. In the middle, DR Congo finished third with 4 points and still dreams: they're in the fight for one of the eight best-third-place spots.
## The qualifiers
Colombia and Portugal completed the mission without needing the head-to-head: their clash, in the final round, ended 0-0. With that, Néstor Lorenzo's Colombia took the group on points, backed by a firm defense — only 1 goal conceded in three matches. Portugal advances in second with the best goal difference in the group (+5), the legacy of the 5-0 thrashing of Uzbekistan, but left a sense of hesitation by drawing both matches against direct rivals (1-1 with DR Congo and 0-0 with Colombia).
## DR Congo's turnaround
Needing a win to stay alive, DR Congo did their part and in dramatic fashion. They fell behind against Uzbekistan — Eldor Shomurodov opened the scoring in the 10th minute — but came back to win 3-1 with a show from Yoane Wissa, who scored twice (68' and 90'+1), and Fiston Mayele, who added to the lead in the 78th minute. The result took the Congolese to 4 points and, for the moment, to the top of the provisional ranking of best third-place finishers. It's a comfortable position, but not a definitive one: the spot will only be confirmed when the other groups close out their rounds.
## Verdict
Colombia and Portugal are through, with Colombia on top; Uzbekistan eliminated without a point in their campaign. DR Congo, third with 4 points, is provisionally inside among the eight best third-place finishers — alive and well positioned, but still dependent on what happens in the other groups. No elimination for the Congolese: the dream remains standing.

L.text:
## Overview
Group L is closed and sent two through to the knockouts. England finished on top with 7 points (2W, 1D) and the best goal difference in the group (+4); Croatia advanced in second with 6 (2W, 1L). Ghana finished third with 4 points and remains alive in the fight for a best-third-place spot. Panama bows out at the bottom, eliminated without a point and without finding the net in three matches.
## The qualifiers
England confirmed their favorite status from the opener, when they got past Croatia 4-2 — Harry Kane scored twice, with Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford completing the tally. They then held a 0-0 against Ghana and closed out with a 2-0 over Panama (Bellingham and Kane again), first place without a scare. Luka Modrić's Croatia digested the opening stumble and reacted as they had to: they beat Panama 1-0 and overcame Ghana 2-1, with Petar Sučić opening the way and Nikola Vlašić sealing the win in the 83rd minute. Runners-up and qualified.
## Ghana still alive
Ghana came out of the group stage with the feeling of a job half done. They opened by beating Panama 1-0, with a goal from Caleb Yirenkyi in stoppage time (90'+5), and grabbed a precious 0-0 against England, but the 2-1 loss to Croatia (Derrick Luckassen scored Ghana's goal) left them in third, with 4 points and a goal difference of zero. Under the rules, the eight best third-place finishers advance, and Ghana appears provisionally inside that cut — an alive position, but still dependent on what the third-place finishers of the other groups do. No elimination: qualification remains open.
## Verdict
England and Croatia qualified, with England's top spot secured on goal difference; Panama eliminated, without a point or a goal scored. Ghana, third with 4 points, is provisionally among the eight best third-place finishers — alive and in the race, but with the spot still pending the outcome of the other groups.
